CNc1nc(C)cc(C(=O)N2CCC[C@H](Cc3cc(OC)ccc3)C2)n1

The fluorophenyl replaced by methoxyphenyl to increase QED and potentially improve binding, maintain SA low due to simple substitution.

This balances increased QED and binding with simpler synthesis.

- The first molecule has a bigger fused heterocycle system.
- The second molecule has fluorines that might improve binding and metabolic stability, but it seems more synthetically complex (higher SA?).

To propose a better molecule:

- Retain the methoxyphenyl group for hydrophobic and solubility properties.
- Introduce one fluorine for improved binding and metabolic stability.
- Combine the fused heterocycle of the first molecule (for higher binding affinity) but simplify substituents to reduce SA.
- Replace the amino substituent on the fused system by a fluorine, to balance polarity and reduce synthetic accessibility.
